23: This study optimized the energy consumption, environmental impacts and cost of arecanut production system through integra- tion of data envelopment analysis and life cycle assessment. The information regarding inputs and out were collected from seventy arecanut farm in Goa state of India. The results indicated that the arecanut production system consumed total energy input of 61706 MJ ha with a total energy output of 75146 MJ ha The consumption of indirect (76%) and renewable energy (53%) was found higher in the arecanut production system. The mean scale efficiency of inefficient farms was found to be 0.87, indicating scope for improvement of management practices to increase the energy use efficiency. Under present condition, the global warming po- tential and non-renewable energy consumption for one-tonne production of arecanut were estimated to be 959.87 kg CO2 eq and 12122.69 M respectively. The energy optimization results indicated a good opportunity to reduce the energy consumption, environmental impacts and cost of arecanut production. In this regard, about 11% of the resources could be saved with a total economic saving of 413 $ ha year without reducing the are- canut yield. It shows that the energy optimization improves the efficiency of arecanut farms in economic perspective. The on-farm emission, nitrogenous fertilizer, and electricity were the main contributor in the majority of the impact categories. Arecanut production had the highest negative impact on human health fol- lowed by ecosystem quality. The efficient use of inputs in synchrony with crop requirement, adoption of drip irrigation, and effective machinery usage should be given priority to reduce the environ- mental impact of arecanut production system in the region.

LCA + DEA technique can be utilized for energy optimization and for environmental impact assessment in different production systems. Hence LCA + DEA methodology can be adopted by agri- cultural departments, policy makers, administrators, and re- searchers as a useful management tool to support decision making.

LCA and DEA may helpful to establish the critical values for envi- ronmental regulations in different agricultural production systems. To conclude, further research is required to harness the full po-

24: This study optimized the energy consumption, environmental impacts and cost of arecanut production system through integra- tion of data envelopment analysis and life cycle assessment. The information regarding inputs and out were collected from seventy arecanut farm in Goa state of India. The results indicated that the arecanut production system consumed total energy input of 61706 MJ ha with a total energy output of 75146 MJ ha The consumption of indirect (76%) and renewable energy (53%) was found higher in the arecanut production system. The mean scale efficiency of inefficient farms was found to be 0.87, indicating scope for improvement of management practices to increase the energy use efficiency. Under present condition, the global warming po- tential and non-renewable energy consumption for one-tonne production of arecanut were estimated to be 959.87 kg CO2 eq and 12122.69 M respectively. The energy optimization results indicated a good opportunity to reduce the energy consumption, environmental impacts and cost of arecanut production. In this regard, about 11% of the resources could be saved with a total economic saving of 413 $ ha year without reducing the are- canut yield. It shows that the energy optimization improves the efficiency of arecanut farms in economic perspective. The on-farm emission, nitrogenous fertilizer, and electricity were the main contributor in the majority of the impact categories. Arecanut production had the highest negative impact on human health fol- lowed by ecosystem quality. The efficient use of inputs in synchrony with crop requirement, adoption of drip irrigation, and effective machinery usage should be given priority to reduce the environ- mental impact of arecanut production system in the region.

LCA + DEA technique can be utilized for energy optimization and for environmental impact assessment in different production systems. Hence LCA + DEA methodology can be adopted by agri- cultural departments, policy makers, administrators, and re- searchers as a useful management tool to support decision making.

LCA and DEA may helpful to establish the critical values for envi- ronmental regulations in different agricultural production systems. To conclude, further research is required to harness the full po-

25: This study optimized the energy consumption, environmental impacts and cost of arecanut production system through integra- tion of data envelopment analysis and life cycle assessment. The information regarding inputs and out were collected from seventy arecanut farm in Goa state of India. The results indicated that the arecanut production system consumed total energy input of 61706 MJ ha with a total energy output of 75146 MJ ha The consumption of indirect (76%) and renewable energy (53%) was found higher in the arecanut production system. The mean scale efficiency of inefficient farms was found to be 0.87, indicating scope for improvement of management practices to increase the energy use efficiency. Under present condition, the global warming po- tential and non-renewable energy consumption for one-tonne production of arecanut were estimated to be 959.87 kg CO2 eq and 12122.69 M respectively. The energy optimization results indicated a good opportunity to reduce the energy consumption, environmental impacts and cost of arecanut production. In this regard, about 11% of the resources could be saved with a total economic saving of 413 $ ha year without reducing the are- canut yield. It shows that the energy optimization improves the efficiency of arecanut farms in economic perspective. The on-farm emission, nitrogenous fertilizer, and electricity were the main contributor in the majority of the impact categories. Arecanut production had the highest negative impact on human health fol- lowed by ecosystem quality. The efficient use of inputs in synchrony with crop requirement, adoption of drip irrigation, and effective machinery usage should be given priority to reduce the environ- mental impact of arecanut production system in the region.

LCA + DEA technique can be utilized for energy optimization and for environmental impact assessment in different production systems. Hence LCA + DEA methodology can be adopted by agri- cultural departments, policy makers, administrators, and re- searchers as a useful management tool to support decision making.

LCA and DEA may helpful to establish the critical values for envi- ronmental regulations in different agricultural production systems. To conclude, further research is required to harness the full po-

26: This study optimized the energy consumption, environmental impacts and cost of arecanut production system through integra- tion of data envelopment analysis and life cycle assessment. The information regarding inputs and out were collected from seventy arecanut farm in Goa state of India. The results indicated that the arecanut production system consumed total energy input of 61706 MJ ha with a total energy output of 75146 MJ ha The consumption of indirect (76%) and renewable energy (53%) was found higher in the arecanut production system. The mean scale efficiency of inefficient farms was found to be 0.87, indicating scope for improvement of management practices to increase the energy use efficiency. Under present condition, the global warming po- tential and non-renewable energy consumption for one-tonne production of arecanut were estimated to be 959.87 kg CO2 eq and 12122.69 M respectively. The energy optimization results indicated a good opportunity to reduce the energy consumption, environmental impacts and cost of arecanut production. In this regard, about 11% of the resources could be saved with a total economic saving of 413 $ ha year without reducing the are- canut yield. It shows that the energy optimization improves the efficiency of arecanut farms in economic perspective. The on-farm emission, nitrogenous fertilizer, and electricity were the main contributor in the majority of the impact categories. Arecanut production had the highest negative impact on human health fol- lowed by ecosystem quality. The efficient use of inputs in synchrony with crop requirement, adoption of drip irrigation, and effective machinery usage should be given priority to reduce the environ- mental impact of arecanut production system in the region.

LCA + DEA technique can be utilized for energy optimization and for environmental impact assessment in different production systems. Hence LCA + DEA methodology can be adopted by agri- cultural departments, policy makers, administrators, and re- searchers as a useful management tool to support decision making.

LCA and DEA may helpful to establish the critical values for envi- ronmental regulations in different agricultural production systems. To conclude, further research is required to harness the full po-

27: This study optimized the energy consumption, environmental impacts and cost of arecanut production system through integra- tion of data envelopment analysis and life cycle assessment. The information regarding inputs and out were collected from seventy arecanut farm in Goa state of India. The results indicated that the arecanut production system consumed total energy input of 61706 MJ ha with a total energy output of 75146 MJ ha The consumption of indirect (76%) and renewable energy (53%) was found higher in the arecanut production system. The mean scale efficiency of inefficient farms was found to be 0.87, indicating scope for improvement of management practices to increase the energy use efficiency. Under present condition, the global warming po- tential and non-renewable energy consumption for one-tonne production of arecanut were estimated to be 959.87 kg CO2 eq and 12122.69 M respectively. The energy optimization results indicated a good opportunity to reduce the energy consumption, environmental impacts and cost of arecanut production. In this regard, about 11% of the resources could be saved with a total economic saving of 413 $ ha year without reducing the are- canut yield. It shows that the energy optimization improves the efficiency of arecanut farms in economic perspective. The on-farm emission, nitrogenous fertilizer, and electricity were the main contributor in the majority of the impact categories. Arecanut production had the highest negative impact on human health fol- lowed by ecosystem quality. The efficient use of inputs in synchrony with crop requirement, adoption of drip irrigation, and effective machinery usage should be given priority to reduce the environ- mental impact of arecanut production system in the region.

LCA + DEA technique can be utilized for energy optimization and for environmental impact assessment in different production systems. Hence LCA + DEA methodology can be adopted by agri- cultural departments, policy makers, administrators, and re- searchers as a useful management tool to support decision making.

LCA and DEA may helpful to establish the critical values for envi- ronmental regulations in different agricultural production systems. To conclude, further research is required to harness the full po-

28: This study optimized the energy consumption, environmental impacts and cost of arecanut production system through integra- tion of data envelopment analysis and life cycle assessment. The information regarding inputs and out were collected from seventy arecanut farm in Goa state of India. The results indicated that the arecanut production system consumed total energy input of 61706 MJ ha with a total energy output of 75146 MJ ha The consumption of indirect (76%) and renewable energy (53%) was found higher in the arecanut production system. The mean scale efficiency of inefficient farms was found to be 0.87, indicating scope for improvement of management practices to increase the energy use efficiency. Under present condition, the global warming po- tential and non-renewable energy consumption for one-tonne production of arecanut were estimated to be 959.87 kg CO2 eq and 12122.69 M respectively. The energy optimization results indicated a good opportunity to reduce the energy consumption, environmental impacts and cost of arecanut production. In this regard, about 11% of the resources could be saved with a total economic saving of 413 $ ha year without reducing the are- canut yield. It shows that the energy optimization improves the efficiency of arecanut farms in economic perspective. The on-farm emission, nitrogenous fertilizer, and electricity were the main contributor in the majority of the impact categories. Arecanut production had the highest negative impact on human health fol- lowed by ecosystem quality. The efficient use of inputs in synchrony with crop requirement, adoption of drip irrigation, and effective machinery usage should be given priority to reduce the environ- mental impact of arecanut production system in the region.

LCA + DEA technique can be utilized for energy optimization and for environmental impact assessment in different production systems. Hence LCA + DEA methodology can be adopted by agri- cultural departments, policy makers, administrators, and re- searchers as a useful management tool to support decision making.

LCA and DEA may helpful to establish the critical values for envi- ronmental regulations in different agricultural production systems. To conclude, further research is required to harness the full po-

tential of LCA + DEA in other agricultural production systems particularly in west coast of India.|sustainability of silage corn production by data envelopment analysis and multi-functional ecological footprint: evidence from sarayan county, Iran.
